# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Set the entrypoint of the container
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash"]

# Add a user with elevated permissions to the container
RUN adduser --uid 1000 --gid 1000 --shell /bin/bash --gecos 'User,None' --disabled-password --home /home/user user

# Set the default user of the container to the newly created user
USER user

# Allow the container to interact with the host's Docker daemon
ENV DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/docker.sock

# Copy the Docker Compose file into the container
COPY docker-compose.yml /

# Run the Docker Compose command to start the containers
RUN cd / && docker-compose up -d

# Expose port 80 for HTTP traffic and 443 for HTTPS traffic
EXPOSE 80 443

# Set the working directory of the container to be /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the app source code into the container
COPY . /app

# Install dependencies using pip
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Run the Django development server in the container
CMD ["python", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"]
